title: Serious accident at the Tour de France: car crashes into barriers at the finish in Correze, leaving eight injured, one seriously

news.title: Serious accident at the Tour de France: car crashes into barriers at the finish in Correze, leaving eight injured, one seriously

world.title: Serious accident at the Tour de France: car crashes into barriers at the finish in Correze, leaving eight injured, one seriously

environment.compositionIntent: Quiet high-key matte paper surround. The environment stays technical support while allowing dark blue side weights, red-brown route contours, ovals, triangular openings, and concentrated fracture pressure to own the visual meaning.

distanceSafety.reason: Canonical public camera retained for the first fixed design bookmark; accepted parts are broad abstract fields and no full-world desktop/mobile preview evidence yet proves a custom projection is required.

contrast.notes: Warm paper light keeps the pale washes visible while a restrained cool rim separates dark saturated blue and near-black anchoring planes without adding spectacle.

owns: renderer compatibility notes for later parts

claiming skybox, shell, or background dominance without evidence

facts: no single centered object
no literal barrier wall
no fixed legacy foreground/background split
no glossy modeled architecture

plannedPart.intent: Build the main all-over internal construction from large geometric planes, horizontal bars, diagonals, loops, vertical side weights, and dark anchoring zones.
plannedPart.messagePurpose: Build the main all-over internal construction from large geometric planes, horizontal bars, diagonals, loops, vertical side weights, and dark anchoring zones.
plannedPart.visualTarget: Build the main all-over internal construction from large geometric planes, horizontal bars, diagonals, loops, vertical side weights, and dark anchoring zones.
plannedPart.relationshipToWhole: This is the skeletal ordering system that makes every other part feel intentional: fractures interrupt it, contours travel along it, voids open inside it, and material skin unifies it.

facts: the accident is not depicted directly

facts: no metallic, glass, plastic, or wet-surface realism
no stock texture asset dependency
no generic bloom/glow as a substitute for material tactility
visibleAbsence.rationale: The material authority explicitly requires matte handmade surfaces and warns against generic 3D material defaults.

plannedPart.intent: Unify planes, contours, voids, and fracture elements with matte painted/printed surface behavior: visible brush drag, thin washes, opaque fills, scraped passages, drips, and uneven hand-laid edges.
plannedPart.messagePurpose: Unify planes, contours, voids, and fracture elements with matte painted/printed surface behavior: visible brush drag, thin washes, opaque fills, scraped passages, drips, and uneven hand-laid edges.
plannedPart.visualTarget: Unify planes, contours, voids, and fracture elements with matte painted/printed surface behavior: visible brush drag, thin washes, opaque fills, scraped passages, drips, and uneven hand-laid edges.
plannedPart.relationshipToWhole: It makes the world feel physically painted rather than digitally assembled, and it keeps all creative systems in the Bram van Velde material register.

facts: animation may be absent or minimal
no literal moving vehicles or cyclists
no heavy bloom, smoke, sparks, or cinematic accident effects
still frame must carry the artwork without motion
visibleAbsence.rationale: Motion is optional by policy and should serve the artist-led construction, not replace it.

plannedPart.intent: Provide deep-atmospheric recession and optional steady-grid-pulse cadence that expresses measured, concentrated pressure while keeping the still composition readable.

plannedPart.visualTarget: Provide deep-atmospheric recession and optional steady-grid-pulse cadence that expresses measured, concentrated pressure while keeping the still composition readable.
plannedPart.relationshipToWhole: It makes the shallow painting language immersive by spacing layers through the camera and adding restrained cadence only where it clarifies the news weighting.

compositionEvidence.draftIntent: Assemble seven accepted WebGPU-compatible Bram van Velde Tour-fracture modules as one all-over abstract field: environment as quiet paper support, asymmetrical-plane-armature as the main structure, concentrated-fracture-transfer as the selected-news pressure, contour/void/material parts as readable overlays, and measured-grid-pulse-depth as subdued recession.
rendererCompatibilityPreserved: No module source was created or replaced.
Every accepted part keeps its accepted moduleRef hash and empty assetRefs.
Accepted WebGPU facts remain attached to the referenced generated modules and accepted-part evidence; composition uses only world/camera/lighting/output grade and part params.

takeaways: Use the canonical fixed camera as the design bookmark and judge both desktop and mobile before changing projection.
Keep color ownership in world.outputColorTransform with one subtle final contrast/saturation/exposure/vignette grade.

Accept only after Playwright desktop and mobile captures plus render reports show nonblank WebGPU output with value range, focal contrast, edge separation, material marks, and controlled accent energy.

image.altText: Serious accident at the Tour de France: car crashes into barriers at the finish in Correze, leaving eight injured, one seriously by Bram van Velde.
